Title: Code DXB - Q1 2026 - Social Media & Marketing Strategy
strategy:
Code DXB - Q1 2026 Social Media Strategy

Stage 1 (January - March 2026)

 

Position Code DXB as the UAE’s definitive guide to practical AI adoption, targeting HR, L&D, enterprise leaders, and tech solution providers. The content will educate, debunk myths, and convert curiosity into actionable adoption. Social media will act as the pre-event knowledge hub, registration driver, and ecosystem builder.

 

Target Audience

  • Enterprise leaders: C-suite, Directors, Department Heads: decision-makers for AI adoption.
  • HR & L&D professionals: Decision-makers in talent and learning functions.
  • Digital transformation & IT teams: AI implementation facilitators.
  • Government & policy influencers: Regulators and AI governance stakeholders.
  • AI & tech vendors: B2B solution providers targeting enterprise adoption.

 

Behavioural drivers: curious, forward-thinking, fast decision-making, professional, solution-driven.

 

Posting Frequency:

January: 4 Reels + 4 Statics

February-March: 2 Reels + 2 Statics

 

Primary Goal:

  • In January, the focus will be on brand awareness, with content designed to educate the audience and drive engagement.
  • In February and March, the focus will shift to brand promotion (Why Exhibit, Call for Speakers, Brochure).

 

Content Pillars:

1. AI Awareness & Education

Goal: Help the audience understand AI’s practical applications and clear confusion around adoption.

 

Formats:

Reels (question-based, POV), Carousels (step-by-step guides, myth-busting).

 

Content Focus:

Ask questions that reveal AI challenges; share practical insights; present frameworks and checklists.

 

2. Credibility & Case Studies

Goal: Build trust by showcasing real-world AI adoption examples and measurable business outcomes.

 

Formats:

LinkedIn Articles, Carousel explainers.

 

Content Focus: Regulatory guidance, frameworks, practical tips for safe and effective AI implementation, HR, L&D, and enterprise success stories; statistics and metrics; expert quotes.

 

3. Exhibitor & Event Promotion

Goal: Position Code DXB as the premier platform to exhibit AI solutions to decision-makers.

 

Formats:

Reels (benefits, ROI, attendee profiles), Carousels (3 Reasons to Exhibit, exhibitor testimonials)

 

Content Focus: “Why Exhibit?”, showcasing audience types, urgency-driven registration.

Title: Elite Property DXB - November 2025 - Social Media & Website Blog Strategy
strategy:
Overview

Starting this November, our social media performance will shift from focusing on month-over-month percentage increases to maintaining a consistent KPI range across platforms for 6 months and then evaluated thereafter based on the previous 6 months performance.

 

The account has now reached a stable and mature growth stage, meaning the priority is consistency in reach, engagement, and content quality rather than chasing spikes each month.

 

By tracking within a healthy performance range, we ensure Elite’s content maintains steady visibility and engagement that reflects the brand’s established authority in the Dubai luxury real estate market.

 

November Objectives

November’s strategy focuses on depth over volume, building stronger emotional resonance through human-led storytelling, property lifestyle narratives, and credible market insight.

We will continue positioning Elite as the benchmark for premium Dubai real estate while strengthening its presence among both buyers and agents through authentic, aspirational, and insight-driven content.

 

Core Goals

  • Maintain consistent reach and engagement across all platforms within the defined KPI range.
  • Drive community engagement through storytelling and agent-focused content.
  • Continue blending lifestyle, property, and human elements to strengthen brand trust.
  • Reinforce Elite’s image as a desirable brand for both clients and aspiring agents.

 

Theme

Beyond the Property: Lifestyle, People, and Perspective

 

Elite continues to represent more than just real estate. It is about lifestyle, ambition, and the people behind the brand.

 

Core Content Pillars

 

1. Lifestyle Storytelling

Why: Buyers connect with lifestyle, not listings.
How: Create immersive, cinematic content that captures the feeling of Dubai living.

 

2. Life at Elite (Culture & Growth)

Why: Humanising the brand makes it relatable and aspirational, especially for new agents.
How: Showcase team culture, achievements, and real stories from within Elite.

 

3. Educational Insights

Why: Knowledge builds credibility and credibility builds trust.
How: Deliver short, informative insights in modern, minimalist formats.

 

4. Agent-Focused and Area Specialist Reels

Why: Reinforce expertise and personal branding for Elite agents.
How: Position agents as local experts who own their area.

 

5. Work with Elite (Recruitment Content)

Why: Build credibility and attract quality agents to join the brand.
How: Present Elite as a growth-driven, empowering, and high-performing workplace.

 

6. CSR and Community Activities

Why: A brand that gives back earns trust and emotional connection.
How: Highlight Elite’s participation in social, charity, and industry events.

 

7. Agent Spotlight

Why: Celebrate individuality and success within the team.
How: Feature one agent per week across stories and posts.

 

Summary

In November, Elite’s strategy will:

  • Prioritise consistency and quality over fluctuation.
  • Focus on storytelling that sells lifestyle and credibility.
  • Strengthen Elite’s internal culture and agent brand visibility.
  • Maintain performance within healthy KPI ranges that reflect a stable, growing brand.

 

Elite’s story continues beyond the property.

Stage 1 (Nov 17 - April 2026): Brand Awareness Campaign

 

Strategy Overview

A 6-month "Problem-First" narrative campaign on LinkedIn. Our strategy will lead with the pressing, unresolved questions in the HR and Education sectors regarding AI. Each month will systematically build from identifying the problem, exploring the consequences, hinting at solutions, and finally, introducing Code DXB as the essential answer. The single weekly post will be high-value and high-impact to cut through the corporate noise.

 

Goal: Build a recognised and trusted brand identity for Code DXB as the definitive thought leader on practical AI application in HR and Education.

 

Target Audience

Primary: C-Suite Executives, VPs of HR & L&D, University Deans, and Chief Innovation Officers in the UAE and wider GCC ("Rule-setters & Decision-makers").

Secondary: HR Managers, Tech Directors, Academic Department Heads, and AI Implementation Specialists ("Industry Specialists").

 

November - December 2025: The Diagnosis

Focus: Articulating the core anxieties and operational challenges.

 

Themes: "Is our workforce obsolete?", "The true cost of ignoring GenAI in curriculum", "Data points you're overlooking in your HR stack."

 

Formats:

  • Carousel Posts: For deep-dives into data and frameworks (e.g., "5 HR Policies Killing AI Adoption").
  • Industry News/Static Posts: Breaking AI news in the region, using a bold graphic and a strong, opinionated caption.
  • Reels/Videos: 15-30 second insights to highlight a key industry problem with on-screen text.
  • Linkedin Articles: Case Studies/Report to offer tangible proof and gated, high-value content to capture leads.

Title: Mutant 2025 - August Social Media & Website Blog Strategy
Canterra Properties - Q1 2026 - Social Media Strategy
strategy:
Canterra’s social presence will be built deliberately, prioritising identity, consistency, and authority before any performance-driven growth. The focus in Q1 is to establish a distinct visual and editorial language that defines how the brand looks, speaks, and shows up across every touchpoint in the market.

 

Content Deliverables:

3 reels per month

2 statics per month

 

Core Content Pillars:

1. Brand Identity & Positioning: This pillar establishes Canterra’s minimalist and refined brand signature, clearly defining how the brand looks, speaks, and presents itself. Content focuses on communicating core brand values, introducing the Four Cs, highlighting visual identity moments, and reinforcing foundational brand statements that set the tone for long-term consistency.

 

Goal: To achieve instant market recognition by owning a distinct, minimalist visual signature.

 

Reels:

  • The Four Cs: A rhythmic edit using clean typography overlays to define Character, Clarity, Commitment, and Consistency.
  • Color & Texture: A visually driven "mood board" reel that showcases the Charcoal, Orange, and Clay palette through architectural and lifestyle clips.

 

Statics:

  • Foundational Statement: A "Quiet Perspective" post featuring the statement: "Not just a better home. A better fit."
  • The Icon Breakdown: A high-contrast graphic of the brand mark with a minimal caption explaining the geometry of the four interlocking Cs.
  • Design Philosophy: A light-driven architectural shot with a small, centered text overlay explaining why Canterra is "Design-led."

 

2. Trust & Credibility: This pillar reinforces confidence through transparency and consistency, strengthening trust between Canterra and its audience. Content includes founder insights, clear explanations of processes, transaction philosophies, and messaging that consistently places client needs and long-term outcomes at the centre.

 

Goal: To establish Canterra as a "Trusted Advisor" rather than just another brokerage. Build long-term brand equity by humanising the expertise and proving the commitment to transparent, knowledge-first guidance.

 

Reels:

About Canterra: A documentary-style reel, discussing the vision behind Canterra and the shift toward "Properties with Purpose."

 

Statics:

Client Philosophy: A quote-style post focused on the "Commitment" pillar, detailing how we protect client interests through every stage of the transaction.

 

Notes:

  • Typography: Strictly F37 Ginger Pro.
  • Negative Space: Ensure all statics feel "airy" and editorial.
  • Logo Usage: Adhere to the Clearspace rules (height of the 'C') to maintain a premium feel.
  • Tone: Always serious, confident, and bold.

Title: Airstay Holiday Homes - August 2025 - Social Media Strategy
strategy:
Objective: Relaunch social media presence after pausing in April, with a content mix focused on acquiring 80% new hosts and appealing to 20% guests

Primary Platforms: Instagram (Reels, Carousels, Stories), LinkedIn (for host acquisition and credibility)

 

Target Audience 1: New Hosts (80%)

 

Goal: Encourage property owners to list with Airstay by highlighting ease, income potential, and full-service support

 

Core Content Pillars:

  • Host Education
    How the process works
    Step-by-step onboarding
    Common misconceptions and FAQs
  • Earning Potential
    Real examples of monthly earnings
    What one-bedroom, two-bedroom, or villa owners can expect
  • Dubai Market Insights
    Trends in short-term rentals
    Why demand is high for holiday homes
    Off-plan property owners converting to short lets
  • Trust and Support
    Testimonials from current hosts
    Behind-the-scenes of property management, guest services
    Airstay as a hands-off, professional partner

 

Recommended Formats:

  • Reels: “What you can earn,” “How onboarding works,” “Why owners are switching to short-term”
  • Carousels: “5 steps to get started,” “Top FAQs for new hosts,” “How we manage your property”
  • Stories: Polls, quick tips, AMAs, day-in-the-life
  • LinkedIn: Host success stories, market commentary, service breakdowns

 

Target Audience 2: Guests (20%)

 

Goal: Drive bookings and stay enquiries by showcasing quality properties and the Dubai lifestyle.

 

Core Content Pillars:

  • Property Highlights
    Featured listings
    New or seasonal stays
    High-performing units
  • Lifestyle and Location
    Area guides (Downtown, Marina, Palm etc.)
    Things to do nearby
    Insider travel tips
  • Guest Experience
    Testimonials and reviews
    Professional housekeeping, concierge services
    What to expect from an Airstay property

Recommended Formats:

  • Reels: “Tour this home,” “Perfect for a long weekend,” “Inside our best-rated listing”
  • Stories: “Book now” features, property countdowns, travel tip series
  • Carousels: “3 places to stay this August,” “Top cafés near your Airstay,” “Neighbourhood spotlight”

 

Stories:

  • Market Insights - 2x
  • Engagement - 4x
  • Reviews or Property - 1x
  • Book Now - 1x

Title: Airstay Holiday Homes - October 2025 - Social Media Strategy
strategy:
October marks the beginning of Dubai's peak tourism season. Our strategy is designed to capture this demand by targeting two audiences with a balanced content mix focused on acquiring new hosts (60%) and guest bookings (40%). Our focus is on creating and emphasising high-engagement formats like reels and stories to inspire staycations and travel, as well as establish Airstay AE as the most trusted and professional property management partner in the UAE.

 

Content Formats

Video Content - 4

Static/Carousels - 4
Stories - 8

Blogs - 5

 

Target Audience 1: Host (60%)

As the current content mix lacks educational material for potential hosts, the aim is to share our expertise and highlight why Airstay is a reliable partner for managing their properties.

 

Content Pillars:

1. Behind the Airstay Experience: Build trust with behind-the-scenes glimpses of the team and seamless management processes.

Goal: Demystify our service and build trust by showcasing our professional, systematic approach to property management.

Formats:

Reel: guest check-in prep, packing welcome kits/personal touches, before and after property transformation

Example: "From Vacant to Welcoming: Our 7-Step Process." "Spend the Day with me as an Airstay staff"

 

2. Timothy's Take: Long-form insights from Timothy about property management to reinforce authority with UAE market data, expert tips on seasonal trends, and analytical takes on holiday let dynamics.

Goal: Attract savvy property investors by providing valuable, data-driven reasons to choose Airstay.

Formats:

LinkedIn articles: market trends and personal insights, positioning Airstay as a voice of authority

 

Target Audience 2: Guest (40%)

We'll create inspiring, story-driven videos that showcase amazing Airstay experiences. The focus will be on the journeys and insights of the guests: the goal is to make people dream about their next holiday.

 

Content Pillars:

1. Aspirational Property Tours: High-quality, cinematic video tours of properties

Goal: Showcasing the lifestyle, not just the space.

Formats:

Reel: Guest POV stay, walkthrough, a day in a life, property showcases

Example: “POV: You woke up in Marina to this view"

 

2. Seasonal Travel Guides: Curated, seasonal, and in-house travel hacks and booking tips. The content should focus on outdoor activities, events, and the pleasant weather.

Goal: Position Airstay as the go-to expert for Dubai travel, capturing search intent related to "things to do in Dubai in October".

Formats:

Blog: highlight upcoming events and attractions, insider tips on navigating busy seasons, while plugging Aistay-managed stays as the solution

Example: "Global Village Reopening: Your Ultimate October Weekend Itinerary in Dubai". "The Secret to a Hassle-Free Staycation in Dubai."

 

3. The Story Behind the Stay/Community: Testimonials from guests, highlighting exceptional stays.

Goal: Build trust and social proof through authentic, relatable experiences from past guests.

Formats:

Carousel: showcase top reviews, focus on the seamless booking process, the perfect location, and the amenities.

Example: "What Guests Love Most About Staying with Airstay"

 

Recommendation:

1. Increase reels output to at least 4 this month as it was our top-performing format in September.

2. To strengthen Airstay's brand authority, we begin publishing blogs and LinkedIn articles.

Title: Elite Property DXB - October 2025 - Social Media & Website Blog Strategy
strategy:
October Objectives

October’s strategy blends aspirational lifestyle content with credible market insights and human storytelling. Reels and introducing agent-focused + recruitment content, Elite will expand reach, engage wider audiences, and strengthen its brand both for buyers and potential agents.

 

  1. Replicate and scale high-performing September content (story-led lifestyle reels + Elite culture).
  2. Significantly increase reach, views, and engagement across platforms while maintaining steady follower growth.
    • IG minimum: 5k views per reel
    • TikTok minimum: 10k views per reel
    • LinkedIn minimum: 2k views per video
  3. Blend aspirational storytelling with credibility to strengthen brand trust.
  4. Continue positioning Elite as a premium brand real estate broker for buyers & agents.

 

Theme

Beyond the Property: Lifestyle, People, and Perspective

 

Core Content Pillars

 

1. Lifestyle Storytelling

Why: Buyers aspire to experiences, not square footage.
How: Cinematic walk-throughs, community narratives, moments of “life in Dubai” woven into property showcases.


Formats:

  • Reels: Morning coffee balcony POVs, park walks, city skyline living.
  • Carousel: “Day in the Life” in Palm Jumeirah / Dubai Hills.

 

2. Life at Elite (Culture & Growth)

Why: Humanising the team makes Elite aspirational for new agents and relatable for clients.
How: Share behind-the-scenes, milestones, Elite family culture.


Formats:

  • Reels: Agent transformation stories, Elite team celebrations.
  • Static/Carousel: “Elite Wins of the Month,” CSR highlights, Elite events.

 

3. Educational Insights

Why: Authority = trust.
How: Snackable data and insights in clean, modern formats.


Formats:

  • Reels: Market shifts explained in 30 seconds.
  • Carousel: “3 Fast Facts: Dubai Real Estate in October.”
  • LinkedIn: Longer-form thought pieces from leadership.

 

New October Additions

 

4. Agent Specialised Area Reels

Spotlight agents as local experts (Dubai Marina, Palm Jumeirah, Dubai Hills, etc.). Builds agent authority + connects clients to specialists.

 

5. Work with Elite (Recruitment)

Reels + statics that showcase why agents should join Elite. Think: earnings potential, culture, credibility.

 

6. CSR / Activities

Show Elite’s role beyond property, team building, charity, industry events. Builds employer branding and relatability.

 

7. Agent Spotlight

Reintroduce profiles of top-performing agents. Share wins, growth, personal stories. Adds credibility + aspirational pull.

 

 

Recommendation

Increase Reels output to 8 this month, leveraging the rich content opportunities available within our existing pillars for October.

December is about visibility, trust-building, and camp promotion. Formats:

Title: Airstay Holiday Homes - Q1 2026 - Social Media Strategy
strategy:
Q1 2026 will focus on evergreen destination appeal, discovery-driven travel content, and property-led storytelling, positioning Airstay as the go-to holiday home brand for travellers exploring Dubai.

As travel behaviour shifts from emotional holiday moments to planning, discovery, and experience-seeking, content will inspire guests through neighbourhood guides, curated stays, real guest experiences, and monthly Dubai highlights, while reinforcing Airstay’s premium service and local expertise.

 

Core Goals

  • Position Airstay as a Dubai holiday home expert, not just a booking platform
  • Drive saves and shares through guide-style and discovery content.
  • Showcase property variety across key Dubai neighbourhoods
  • Build trust through UGC and consistent guest-led storytelling
  • Strengthen brand authority with market insight and curated recommendations

 

Deliverables (Monthly Basis)

4 Reels

4 Carousels

8 Stories

 

Core Content Pillars

1. Dubai Neighbourhood Discovery

Goal: Position Airstay as the insider guide to Dubai’s best holiday home locations, helping guests choose the right area based on lifestyle, travel purpose, and vibe.

 

Reels:

Dubai Tourist Guide Series (Discover Dubai)

This mini series spotlights one Dubai neighbourhood at a time, highlighting its vibe, lifestyle, key attractions, and why it’s a great place to stay. Each reel connects the destination experience with nearby Airstay homes.

 

Carousels:

Top 5 Holiday Home Areas in Dubai

 

Notes:

  • Focus on location, nearby attractions, cafés, and views
  • Designed for high saves + shares
  • Clean visuals, map-style overlays, POV shots

 

2. Property Spotlights & Top Picks

Goal: Highlight the quality, variety, and lifestyle appeal of Airstay homes while making it easy for guests to imagine themselves staying there.

 

Carousels:

Property Showcases

Top Picks: Airstay Holiday Homes (Previous Month)

 

Notes:

  • Emphasise design, layout, comfort, and views
  • Keep captions benefit-led, not feature-heavy
  • Ideal for website clicks and booking intent

 

3. Experience-led Dubai Content

Goal: Inspire travel by showing what guests can experience when staying with Airstay, beyond just the accommodation.

 

Reels:

1. This Month in Dubai

A monthly reel series that highlights the key events, festivals, and happenings across Dubai for the upcoming month. Each reel informs guests about what’s on, from cultural experiences to entertainment and seasonal activities, while linking the city’s vibrancy to staying at an Airstay home.

 

2. Must Tries in Dubai (Food, Cafés & Restaurants)

A bite-sized reel series spotlighting Dubai’s must-try food spots—from iconic restaurants to hidden café gems.

 

Notes:

  • Strong hook in first 3 seconds
  • Informative but lifestyle-driven
  • Encourages saves and repeat views

 

4. Guest Stories & User-generated Content

Goal: Build trust and relatability by featuring real guests and authentic moments across different traveller types.

 

Reels:

UGC (Property-focused)

Authentic guest experiences and stories, showcasing real stays, memorable moments, and personal perspectives from travellers at Airstay homes.

 

Notes:

  • Minimal polish, natural tone
  • Strong social proof for bookings
  • Rotate family, solo, couples, and business travellers

 

5. Timothy’s Take

Goal: Strengthen authority and brand credibility by sharing leadership insights and market knowledge.

 

LinkedIn Blogs:

  1. Travel trends for Q1
  2. Guest behaviour insights
  3. Dubai short-term rental outlook

 

Notes:

  • Educational, not promotional
  • Clean, professional tone

Title: Lino Lada - December 2025 - Social Media & Content Strategy

Title: Alkalive - November 2025 - Social Media & Website Blogs Strategy
strategy:
Monthly Deliverables:

Title: Elite Property DXB - December 2025 - Social Media & Website Blog Strategy
strategy:
Overview
Building on November’s strong performance, December will continue to prioritise consistency, emotional storytelling, and high-quality content aligned with Elite’s position in the luxury real estate market.

The goal remains: sustained performance within healthy KPI ranges across all platforms, while reinforcing Elite as a trusted, aspirational, and people-driven brand.

 

Core Objectives

  1. Maintain stable reach and engagement across all platforms in line with November’s benchmarks
  2. Strengthen community connection through storytelling and agent narratives
  3. Position Elite as both a premium real estate brand and a top workplace for ambitious agents
  4. Sustain quality, rhythm, and authenticity through a curated mix of lifestyle, team culture, and educational content

 

Content Pillars (Continued from November)

 

1. Lifestyle Storytelling
Create immersive content that captures Dubai living beyond square footage

2. Life at Elite (Culture & Growth)
Highlight internal culture, achievements, and behind-the-scenes team moments

3. Educational Insights
Share valuable market knowledge and tips in clean, concise formats

4. Agent-Focused & Area Specialist Reels
Reinforce agent presence and authority by showcasing them in the areas they specialise in

5. Work with Elite (Recruitment)
Feature Elite as a workplace for growth-driven, high-performing individuals

6. CSR & Community
If applicable, feature any holiday or community engagement Elite participates in

7. Agent Spotlight
Continue weekly spotlight features to build team visibility and human connection

 

Suggested December Additions

  • 2025 Market Predictions: Feature agent insights about upcoming trends in carousel or reel format
  • Elite Wrapped: Recap wins, milestones, and top content of the year
  • Festive Lifestyle Content: Subtle holiday moments that reflect Dubai’s luxury lifestyle
  • Year-End Agent Appreciation: Celebrate achievements and set the tone for January

Summary

 

December’s strategy is focused on consistency, storytelling, and staying top of mind. By continuing the successful formula from November and layering in seasonal content, Elite can maintain performance and brand equity through the year-end period.

Title: Elite Property DXB - Q1 2026 - Social Media & Website Blogs Strategy
strategy:
Content Strategy Overview

 

For Q1, our approach is to streamline content, boost storytelling, and double down on formats that drove leads and engagement in 2025. With refined focus, each platform will serve a distinct role in the conversion funnel, guiding general audiences down to high-intent clients.

 

Top performing post of 2025 to adopt

  • Culture-Driven Content
    Human-centric content continues to dominate. Plan weekly team reels, behind-the-scenes, celebrations, and lifestyle moments that reflect Elite’s dynamic work culture.
  • Agent Spotlights & Educational Stories
    Bite-sized, value-driven posts showcasing agent wins, area knowledge, and process breakdowns (e.g. “Buying in Dubai 101”, “Golden Visa guides”) — ideally in Reel/Carousel formats.
  • Story-Based Property Tours
    Focus on cinematic, VO-led walkthroughs with emotional narrative. Use YouTube for long-form, Instagram Reels and TikToks for vertical teasers. Highlight lifestyle, not just specs.
  • Relatable Agent Humor & Bloopers
    Lean into authentic moments to balance the feed. Low-lift, high-engagement, especially for TikTok.
  • Cinematic POV Reels
    Enhance these with storytelling — use text overlays, voiceovers or agent commentary. Best for Instagram Reels and TikTok.
  • Salesy Ads / Launch Posts
    These underperformed unless paired with high visuals or value. Repurpose for paid only, supported by Luxebridge. Remove from organic unless it’s a trophy listing.

 

Platform Roles & Funnel Positioning

  • TikTok
    Role:     Branding & Awareness
    Audience: General public, global viewers
    Focus Content: POV reels, Dubai lifestyle, funny agent content, transformations
  • Instagram
    Role:     Discovery & Engagement
    Audience: Homeowners, tenants, investors, agents
    Focus Content: Cinematic property tours, agent reels, educational carousels, UGC, stories
  • LinkedIn
    Role:     Agent Recruitment & Branding
    Audience: Agents, brokers, career switchers
    Focus Content: Team wins, promotions, job openings, thought leadership
  • YouTube
    Role:     Long-Form & Property Immersion
    Audience: Global investors, high-intent buyers
    Focus Content: Chaptered cinematic property tours, Dubai neighbourhood series, educational long-form
  • Facebook
    Role:     Paid Campaigns Only
    Audience: Older, high-net-worth audiences
    Focus Content: Paid ad content only (managed by Luxebridge)

 

Monthly Deliverables

  • 8 Reels/month (culture, tours, agent content, POV)
  • 4 Static Posts/month (carousel tips, spotlights, quotes)
  • 2 Long-form YouTube uploads (property tours, area guides)
  • 4–6 Stories/week with CTAs, behind-the-scenes, polls, UGC

 

Refinements & Experiments

  • Remove static “salesy” listings unless high-ticket or story-led
  • Focus on high-production property videos with VO + narrative
  • Layer in humour + UGC moments to avoid over-curated aesthetic
  • Keep ad-focused assets with Luxebridge for paid only

Title: Sovereign Capital - Q1 2026 - Social Media Marketing & Website Blogs Strategy
strategy:
Sovereign Capital – Main Content Objectives

  • Establish Sovereign Capital as a trusted long-term real estate advisory in Abu Dhabi, not a transactional brokerage, by leading with insight, judgement, and clarity
  • Build investor confidence before the first conversation through consistent, considered content that demonstrates understanding of market fundamentals, risk, and long-term value
  • Position Sovereign Capital as Abu Dhabi specialists, showcasing deep local knowledge of neighbourhoods, culture, and investment dynamics beyond price and listings
  • Communicate authority and credibility without sales pressure, using education-led content that explains how intelligent capital behaves in Abu Dhabi
  • Differentiate Sovereign Capital through its decision-making process, clearly articulating how investments are evaluated, filtered, and prioritised
  • Reflect the Elite Property DXB pedigree while building an independent Abu Dhabi identity, aligned with higher standards, global practices, and long-term relationships
  • Attract serious, high-intent investors who value stability, legacy, discretion, and informed decision-making over speed or speculation
  • Embed legacy as a core strategic theme, reinforcing long-term thinking through consistency, restraint, and a refusal to chase trends
  • Create warmer, more informed sales conversations, where investors arrive educated, aligned, and already familiar with Sovereign’s philosophy
  • Build a brand presence designed to last, prioritising trust, recall, and reputation over volume, virality, or short-term performance metrics

1- What the Content Strategy Is (and What It Is Not)

 

The Role of Content at Sovereign Capital

Sovereign Capital’s content strategy is not designed to generate short-term leads or promote listings.
It exists to build investor confidence, establish authority, and signal long-term intent in Abu Dhabi’s real estate market.

- Content is used as an extension of the advisory process.

- Where most real estate brands communicate availability, Sovereign communicates judgement.

- Where the market speaks in urgency, Sovereign speaks in clarity.

 

Content is how Sovereign earns trust before the first conversation.

 

2- Brand-Aligned Content Principles

All content must consistently reflect the following:

 

Tone of Voice

  • Calm
  • Confident
  • Clear
  • Considered

What we explicitly avoid:
Sales language, urgency, hype, promises, scripted hooks, over-explanation.

Visual Language

  • Editorial framing
  • Real locations, real architecture, real environments
  • Slow pacing, natural light
  • Minimal typography, restrained overlays

Content should feel timeless, not trend led.

 

3. Content Pillars (Strategic Use, Not Volume)

Sovereign’s content pillars are not content “categories” they are signals.
Each pillar exists to communicate something specific to a high-value investor.

 

Pillar 1: Investor Intelligence

Purpose: Authority & credibility
Role: Educate without overwhelming

This pillar explains how intelligent capital behaves in Abu Dhabi.

 

Pillar 2: Provenance & Place

Purpose: Local depth & market truth
Role: Demonstrate understanding of place, not products

This content shows that Sovereign understands Abu Dhabi beyond price per square foot.

 

Pillar 3: The Sovereign Way (Thought Process)

Purpose:Differentiation
Role: Explain how decisions are made

This pillar communicates Sovereign’s internal filters, standards, and discipline.

 

Pillar 4: Foundation, Trust & Legacy

Purpose: Emotional confidence
Role: Build belief without testimonials

This pillar humanises the brand quietly, without performance or sales pressure.

 

4- Legacy as a Strategic Theme (Not a Campaign)

 

Legacy at Sovereign Capital is not expressed through nostalgia or heritage visuals.
It is expressed through restraint, consistency, and long-term thinking.

Legacy is shown by:

  • Repeating core beliefs over time
  • Consistent refusal to chase trends
  • Emphasis on process over outcomes
  • Calm confidence in messaging

This mirrors how legacy investors behave.

 

5. Target Audience (High-Intent, Clearly Defined)

Sovereign Capital does not speak to the mass market.

 

Primary Audiences

5.1- Global Capital Diversifiers

  • International investors seeking AED-pegged stability
  • Comparing Abu Dhabi with London, New York, Singapore
  • Expect data, context, and discretion

5.2- GCC & Local HNWIs

  • Focused on intergenerational wealth
  • Value reputation, relationships, and privacy
  • Interested in stability over speed

5.3- High-Earning Professionals Relocating to Abu Dhabi

  • Tech, finance, leadership roles
  • Seeking clarity in an unfamiliar market
  • Distrust traditional sales agents

Audience Mindset

  • Risk-aware
  • Reputation-driven
  • Long-term oriented
  • Intolerant of hype

Content must feel like it respects their intelligence.

 

6. Strategic Outcome of the Content Strategy

When executed correctly, Sovereign Capital’s content will result in:

  • Warmer, more informed client conversations
  • Investors referencing Sovereign content before meetings
  • Strong brand recall without high posting frequency
  • Credibility that precedes sales discussions

Success looks like trust arriving before demand.

 

7. Final Strategic Positioning

Sovereign Capital’s content strategy positions the brand as:

- A long-term real estate advisory,
- Rooted in Abu Dhabi,
- Built on trust,
- Designed for legacy capital.

 

This is not content designed to be loud.
It is content designed to last.

Title: Crypto Homes DXB - September 2025 - Social Media Strategy
strategy:
For September, our goal is to continue to position Crypto Homes DXB as the go-to authority for crypto-backed property investments, while educating and engaging both crypto enthusiasts and real estate investors.

 

Content Pillars

 

1. Education & Awareness (Crypto + Real Estate Basics)

Goal: Build trust and visibility by simplifying complex ideas.

  • Reels: Quick explainers on crypto terms, blockchain in real estate, FAQs (“What makes a property crypto-ready?”).
  • Static/Carousel: Step-by-step guides (“How to buy property with Bitcoin in Dubai”), infographics on crypto adoption.
  • Stories: Polls and quizzes to test audience knowledge (“Would you buy a villa with crypto?”).

 

2. Market Insights & Trends

Goal: Showcase credibility and thought leadership.

  • Reels: “This week in Dubai real estate & crypto” (bite-sized updates).
  • Static/Carousel: Market snapshot graphics with stats (visitor numbers, property yields, crypto adoption news).
  • LinkedIn: Long-form posts from founders discussing regulation, tokenisation, and investor trends.

 

3. Engagement & Entertainment

Goal: Keep audiences hooked with relatable, viral content.

  • Reels: Quiz and trend-based formats tied to crypto investing (“POV: You bought property with BTC in Dubai”).
  • Stories: AMA sessions, “This or That” style polls (“Palm villa or Marina penthouse?”).

 

4. Jake’s Take: Crypto & Property Unlocked

Goal: Position Jake Jones as a thought leader while giving the audience long-form, conversational value.

  • Engagement Tie-In: Ask listeners to send in questions → answered in future episodes → repurposed as short-form Q&A Reels.

 

5. How to Use the Website (Guides & Walkthroughs)

Goal: Drive traffic to the platform and make it easier for users (buyers, sellers, and partners) to understand and take action on Crypto Homes DXB.

  • Short tutorials like “How to search crypto-ready properties in 20 seconds”.
  • Walkthroughs showing the process of filtering listings by location or crypto payment option.
  • “POV” skits (user browsing → finding a villa → clicking ‘inquire’).
  • Step-by-step graphics: “How to register as a buyer”, “How to list your property”, “How agencies can partner with us”.
  • Screenshots with arrows/highlights to show where to click for key actions.

Title: Crypto Homes DXB - Q1 2026 - Social Media & Website Blog Strategy
strategy:
Crypto Homes DXB - Q1 2026 Social Media Strategy (Jan–Mar)

Quarterly Strategy Overview

 

As Crypto Homes DXB enters its sixth month on social media, Q1 2026 marks a shift from broad awareness into clarity-led, confidence-building content. The focus is on helping audiences understand how crypto real estate works in the UAE, why Crypto Homes DXB is the trusted bridge, and what opportunities are available right now.

 

Core Objectives

  • Strengthen Crypto Homes DXB’s authority in UAE crypto real estate
  • Increase familiarity with the platform and buying process
  • Educate audiences through simplified, founder-led explanations
  • Drive high-intent interest toward available crypto-friendly properties
  • Improve saves, shares, and repeat engagement

 

Content Deliverables (Monthly Basis)

8 reels

8 statics

8 stories

 

Content Pillars

1. Crypto Talks: Expert Insights, Market Trends & Myth-Busting (4 Reels/month)

Goal: Build authority and dismantle the barriers/fears of crypto-real estate investing.

 

Format:

  • 1 long-form landscape video
  • Repurposed into short-form content

 

Talent:

Abeer – Host

Jake & Aaron – Speakers

 

Topics Covered:

  • Introduction: How crypto is transforming real estate in Dubai & Abu Dhabi
  • Why crypto investors are skipping banks to buy property
  • Tenants paying rent in Ethereum
  • Buying off-plan properties with Bitcoin
  • Crypto-funded cafés and offices in Dubai & Abu Dhabi
  • Renting holiday homes with crypto
  • Monthly rent payments using Bitcoin
  • What’s next: Smart cities, CARF 2027, and real-world assets
  • Outro: Final thoughts + how to get started with Crypto Homes DXB

 

Style: Jake or Aaron with a guest when relevant. Conversational, educational, clarity-first, highly saveable.

 

2. How Crypto Homes Works: Platform Walkthroughs, Tutorials & Process (4 Reels/month)

Goal: Educate and convert curiosity into understanding by simplifying the customer journey through showing how Crypto Homes DXB operates.

 

Format:

  • 1 long-form landscape video
  • Repurposed into short-form content

 

Talent:

Jake

 

Topics Covered:

  • How to buy a property in Dubai & Abu Dhabi using crypto
  • How to rent a property in Dubai & Abu Dhabi using crypto (including monthly rent payments)
  • How to buy off-plan property in Dubai & Abu Dhabi using crypto
  • How to buy commercial property in Dubai & Abu Dhabi using crypto
  • How to rent a holiday home in Dubai & Abu Dhabi using crypto
  • How to pay monthly rent in Dubai & Abu Dhabi using crypto

 

Style: Screen-shares, "Step 1-2-3" formats, clarity-first, and professional tutorials.

 

3. Crypto Real Estate News & Trends (4 Statics/month)

Goal: Position Crypto Homes DXB as a reliable source of timely, factual crypto real estate insights.

 

Content Focus:

  • UAE crypto adoption updates
  • Regulatory announcements simplified
  • Market snapshots (crypto ↔ real estate correlations)
  • Crypto real estate facts and data-led insights

 

Format:

Clean visuals, short headlines, concise explanations.

 

4. Top Properties Available Now (BTC / ETH) (4 Carousels/month)

Goal: Drive high-intent interest by showcasing properties and locations that appeal to crypto buyers and investors.

 

Content Focus:

  • Featured properties purchasable with BTC/ETH
  • Key details: location, price, investment angle
  • Lifestyle + investment positioning
  • Clear call-to-action for enquiries

 

Format:

Premium visuals, aspirational but informative, conversion-focused

Title: Airstay Holiday Homes - September 2025 - Social Media Strategy
strategy:
In September, Airstay will consolidate credibility by deepening host trust through education, testimonials, and real earnings examples; differentiate itself from competitors by highlighting full-service support and stress-free hosting; expand host acquisition by focusing on property types such as apartments, villas, and off-plan conversions; keep guest-focused content aspirational but secondary with polished property and lifestyle posts; and boost engagement through interactive formats like polls, AMAs, comparisons, and UGC-style reels to build community.

 

Updated Content Pillars & Angles

 

Target Audience 1: New Hosts (80%)

 

1. Host Education

  • September angle: Move from “how-to start” in August → “how-to succeed” in September.
  • Example: “3 signs your property is ready for short-term letting” or “How to avoid the top 5 mistakes new hosts make.”

2. Earning Potential

  • Push real comparisons: “What this Downtown 1-bedroom earns vs. a Palm villa” → presented as simple visuals or reels.
  • Add “seasonal spike” angle: Highlight how Q4 travel (Oct–Dec) boosts host earnings.

3. Market Insights

  • Focus on September as a lead-in to peak season.
  • Content around Expo-like events, tourism spikes, and the school holiday period.
  • Tie insights directly to host benefits: “Here’s why September is the month to get listed before tourist demand surges.”

4. Trust & Support

  • Showcase more behind-the-scenes: cleaners prepping, guest check-in setup, Airstay staff explaining processes.
  • Add testimonial reels: short host soundbites on ease of use.

 

Target Audience 2: Guests (20%)

 

1. Property Highlights

  • September angle: Feature upcoming seasonal stays (autumn getaways, long weekends).
  • Include “Instagrammable” units (pool, sea views, rooftop terraces).

2. Lifestyle & Location

  • September content: Guides tied to cooler weather → outdoor dining, desert trips, beach season returning.
  • Carousel: “3 Perfect September Getaways in Dubai.”

3. Guest Experience

  • Strong push on professionalism: “What makes an Airstay home different from an Airbnb?”
  • Reviews/testimonials → highlight consistency, cleanliness, and concierge service.

Title: Social Media & Content Strategy - Q1 2026
strategy:
Objectives:

 

Drive consumer conversion through scientific differentiation and lifestyle alignment. By executing targeted educational content that clearly substantiates the superior pH and mineral advantages of Alkalive, we aim to establish the brand as the trusted, premium choice for high-performance hydration and modern wellness routines in Jordan.

 

Following the success of late 2025, the Q1 strategy focuses on renewal and consistency, positioning Alkalive as the essential partner for health-conscious Jordanians starting their new year’s resolutions.

 

This quarter follows this direction:

 

  1. New Year, New Routine: Simplify the science behind alkaline water (pH 8–8.5, minerals, fast absorption) to fit into "fresh start" wellness habits.
  2. SEO & Social Synergy: Align social content subjects with the new Website Blogs and targeted keywords like "alkaline water Jordan" to drive cross-platform traffic.
  3. Holistic Health: Focus on the importance of hydration for immunity and recovery during the final months of winter and the transition into spring

 

Content Deliverables (Monthly):

  • 5 Static Posts/Carousels
  • 1 Reel

 

Content Pillars:

 

1. Education & Differentiation

Goal: To simplify the science behind alkaline water (pH balance, minerals, hydration) and highlight what makes Alkalive a scientifically backed, premium water brand.

 

What’s new this quarter:

  • Deep Dive into pH: Explaining the specific benefits of pH 8–8.5 for balancing body acidity after holiday indulgence.
  • Mineral Bioavailability: Educating the audience on why Alkalive’s mineral profile leads to faster absorption.
  • SEO-Driven Educational Graphics: Infographics based on high-volume search queries regarding "alkaline benefits".

 

2. Lifestyle & Community

Goal: To connect Alkalive to real Jordanian lifestyles, positioning it as an essential part of an aspirational, active, and mindful daily routine.

 

What’s new this quarter:

 

  • "The 2026 Blueprint": Showcasing hydration moments in professional and fitness settings (work, gym, morning routines).
  • Jordanian Settings: Using local landmarks and relatable visuals to build community trust.
  • Interactive Community Challenges: Encouraging followers to share their "Balance" goals for the year.

 

3. Trust & Transparency

Goal: To build credibility and trust by showcasing Alkalive’s quality, purity, and authentic Jordanian roots.

 

What’s new this quarter:

 

  • Behind-the-Source: Content focusing on the Alkalive water source and the rigorous purification process.
  • Premium Look & Feel: Highlighting the aesthetic appeal of the packaging in "lifestyle" environments.
  • Q&A Series: Direct engagement by asking "What keeps followers balanced daily?" and addressing common water-quality myths.

 

4. Seasonal Wellness & Education

Goal: To address season-specific hydration needs, focusing on the importance of staying properly hydrated even during cold weather to support energy and immunity.

 

What’s new this quarter:

 

  • Winter Immunity: Content focusing on how hydration supports the immune system during the peak of flu season.
  • Hydration for Recovery: Focusing on hydration's role in muscle recovery for those starting new January fitness programs.
  • Spring Transition: Shifting visuals from "cozy winter" to "active fresh start" as the quarter progresses.

 

Notes:

To sustain consistency and ensure the brand maintains its bold yet human tone, all creative assets must adhere to the visual goal:

Clean, airy, real-life Jordan moments, diverse, inclusive, and aspirational. The immediate focus remains on Growth, SEO Integration, and Sustained Education.

Title: Crypto Homes DXB - October 2025 - Social Media & Website Blog Strategy
strategy:
This month balances thought leadership, engagement, and direct property promotion. It leans on Jake & Aaron’s credibility, delivers timely market insights, and introduces property-focused content tied directly to the website.

 

Content Formats

Video Content – 8 + 2 (carry over from July)

Image Content – 8
Stories - 8

 

1. Jake’s Take (Crypto & Property Unlocked) – Featuring Jake & Aaron

  • Long-form insights from Jake and Aaron about crypto real estate trends.
  • Formats:
    • Reels with Q&A snippets from both (audience-submitted questions).
    • LinkedIn articles positioning them as thought leaders.
  • Goal: Strengthen credibility, make the brand more personal and authoritative.

 

2. Market Insights & Trends

  • Weekly updates on Dubai real estate + crypto adoption.
  • Formats:
    • Reels: “Crypto News”
    • Carousels: Stats + graphics (e.g., property yields, transaction volumes, crypto adoption rates).
    • Stories: Polls like “Do you think tokenisation is the future of real estate?”
  • Goal: Showcase Crypto Homes as the go-to source for trustworthy news.

 

3. Engagement & Entertainment (Quiz Reels)

  • Keep audiences engaged with interactive formats.
  • Formats:
    • “Myth or Fact” quiz reels.
    • Polls in Stories to test crypto/real estate knowledge.
    • Quick “POV” memes (e.g., “POV: You bought your first villa with Bitcoin”).
  • Goal: Drive comments, shares, and story interactions.

 

New October Additions

 

4. Property Tour Reels

  • Walkthroughs of listed properties to spark curiosity and encourage website visits.
  • Content:
    • Showcase interiors, exteriors, and lifestyle around the property.
  • Goal: Encourage viewing listings on the website.

 

5. Property Carousels (with Crypto Prices)

  • Static posts displaying properties with both AED and crypto price equivalents (BTC).
  • Stories promoting hot listing and links to website
  • Goal: Position Crypto Homes as the hub for crypto property buyers while boosting website traffic.

 

Minimum views per video content (Organic + Boosted):

  • 2k on IG
  • 5k on TikTok
  • 500 on LinkedIn

 

Boosting Strategy

  • Promote all reels initially at AED 50.00 minum, allocate more budget on best performing reels at AED 100.00 per reel.
  • Additional budget allocation to property tour reels at AED 100.00 per reel

For October, the strategy is designed to announce and cement the HORECA 2026 rebrand, clearly communicating its expanded scope: Connecting Hotels, Restaurants & Catering - establishing it as Libya's leading hospitality platform.

 

Content Formats:

6 Reels

8 Stories

 

Theme: Connecting Hotels, Restaurants & Catering Under One Roof

 

Content Pillars:

1. The Rebrand: Announcement of HORECA

Goal: To establish the shift from ‘The RNF Show’ to ‘HORECA’ and position the event as the leading leadership platform for the entire hospitality ecosystem in Libya.

 

2. The Value Proposition: HORECA Libya 2026 Preview

Goal: To answer "What's in it for me?" for the attendees and exhibitors

 

3. The Call to Action: Be Part of It

Goal: To convert the awareness and interest from the first two pillars into registrations

strategy:

Primary Goals

Title: Airstay Holiday Homes - December 2025 - Social Media Strategy
strategy:
For December, our strategy will focus on delivering holiday-themed, emotionally rich, and destination-driven content that positions Airstay as the top choice for travellers. Every piece of content will highlight comfort, family moments, and the magic of spending the season in Dubai.

 

Core Goals:

  • Build emotional connection through festive and family-oriented content
  • Increase engagement and share-worthy posts during peak season
  • Demonstrate Airstay’s value through location, comfort, and guest experience
  • Highlight Dubai as the ideal December destination
  • Strengthen Airstay’s brand authority through leadership insights (Timothy’s Take)

 

Deliverables:

4 Reels

4 Carousels

8 Stories

 

Content Pillars:

1. Holiday-Themed Content

Goal: Create a fun, cosy, and festive atmosphere that encourages guests to choose Airstay for their seasonal stays.

 

Format:

Reels: “Celebrate the Holidays at Airstay Holiday Homes,” “Festive Date Night”

 

2. Dubai as the Perfect Holiday Destination

Goal: Showcase why Dubai is a dream December getaway: iconic locations, festive events, incredible views, and how Airstay completes the experience.

 

Format:

Reel: “A Day in Dubai During the Holidays”

 

3. Family & Togetherness

Goal: Tap into emotional storytelling that highlights family warmth, shared traditions, and holiday bonding inside Airstay homes.

 

Format:

Reel: “Small moments that feel big” (POV lifestyle shots), Emotional storytelling panels (“This is where memories happen…”)

 

4. Timothy’s Take

Goal: Strengthen authority, trust, and brand credibility by sharing leadership perspectives and market insights directly from Timothy.

 

Format:

Blog: Key market trends for December, short insights on guest behaviour and seasonal travel

 

Note:

Maintain cultural sensitivity for the UAE (no religious symbolism beyond general festive visuals)

Title: Calvo - December 2025 - Social Media & Content Strategy
strategy:
Objectives:

Establish decisive market entry and cultural resonance by consistently communicating the brand's authentic Spanish heritage and superior quality, while strategically showcasing its versatility to integrate seamlessly into diverse Jordanian family recipes and everyday meals, thereby driving consumer trial and adoption.

 

Following the launch in November, December's strategy will continue to emphasise Mediterranean quality, authentic Spanish heritage, and visually compelling meal inspiration that highlights Calvo Tuna as the premium-yet-accessible brand for every Jordanian table.

 

This month follows this direction:

  1. Cultural Localization: Blend Calvo’s Spanish soul with local Jordanian culture to drive emotional connection and acceptance (e.g., using Calvo in local favorite meals).
  2. Education & Versatility: Communicate Calvo’s Spanish origins, quality, and versatility as a high-protein, healthy ingredient in daily and family meals.
  3. Engagement & Conversion: Build community through interactive content to encourage trial and drive retail visibility.

 

Content Deliverables:

1 Reel

5 Static/Carousels

4 Stories

 

Content Pillars:

1. Brand & Product Education

Goal: To communicate Calvo’s Spanish origins, quality process, and nutritional superiority, establishing it as a premium-yet-accessible protein partner.

What's new this month:

  • Focus on "From Spain to Jordan" journey and heritage (e.g., "Why Everyone in Spain Loves Calvo").
  • Highlight the high protein, low fat, and Omega-3 benefits using simple infographics.

 

2. Lifestyle & Recipe Inspiration

Goal: To demonstrate Calvo’s versatility by providing easy, healthy, Mediterranean-inspired recipes that fit the modern Jordanian lifestyle.

What's new this month:

  • Showcase recipes that are "Fast, fresh, and full of flavor" (e.g., Tuna Fettuccine Mediterranean Style).
  • Use step-by-step carousels and visually appealing food photography.

 

3. Cultural Localization

Goal: To build connection by seamlessly integrating Calvo into familiar Jordanian meals and flavor profiles.

What's new this month:

  • Focus on "Tuna Your Way" by featuring local favorites (From Maqluba to Tabbouleh) reimagined with Calvo.
  • Leverage cultural touchpoints to show how the brand fits right in.

 

4. Engagement & Community

Goal: To build an interactive community and increase followers by using relatable and fun content.

What's new this month:

  • Implement interactive Stories (Polls, Quizzes) such as "Which Calvo Dish Are You?" or "Guess the Ingredient."
  • Promote conversation with questions encouraging followers to share their favorite tuna meals.

 

Notes:

To sustain the launch momentum and ensure a strong finish to the year, boosted posts should focus on Conversion (product visibility) and Education (nutrition/quality), with a strong emphasis on blending the Spanish heritage with authentic Jordanian flavor and family moments.

Title: Alkalive Jordan - December 2025 - Social Media & Content Strategy
strategy:
Objectives:

Drive consumer conversion through scientific differentiation and lifestyle alignment by executing targeted educational content that clearly substantiates the superior pH and mineral advantages of the water, establishing Alkalive as the trusted, premium choice for high-performance hydration and modern wellness routines.

 

Following November's results, December's strategy will continue to emphasise consistent content, education, and visually compelling posts that highlight Alkalive as the premium alkaline water brand designed to energise modern, health-conscious Jordanians.

 

This month follows this direction:

  1. Educate & Differentiate: Simplify the science behind alkaline water (pH balance, minerals, fast absorption).
  2. Foster Lifestyle Connection: Position Alkalive as part of an aspirational, active, and mindful Jordanian lifestyle.
  3. Address Seasonal Wellness: Focus on the importance of hydration and balance during the winter months.

Content Deliverables:

 

5 Static/Carousels

1 Reel

4 Stories

 

Content Pillars:

 

1. Education & Differentiation

Goal: To simplify the science behind alkaline water (pH balance, minerals, hydration) and highlight what makes Alkalive a scientifically backed, premium water brand.

What's new this month:

  • Explaining Alkalive’s pH & mineral advantage (pH 8–8.5, rich minerals, fast absorption).
  • Side-by-side comparison of alkaline vs. regular water benefits.
  • Minimal infographic style content to communicate science simply.

 

2. Lifestyle & Community

Goal: To connect Alkalive to real Jordanian lifestyles, positioning it as an essential part of an aspirational, active, and mindful lifestyle.

What's new this month:

  • Content showcasing everyday hydration moments in Jordan (work, gym, cafe, commuting).
  • Emphasis on the theme: "This Is What Balance Looks Like."
  • Using local settings and relatable visuals to build community trust.

 

3. Trust & Transparency

Goal: To build credibility and trust by showcasing Alkalive's quality, purity, and authenticity.

What's new this month:

  • Content focusing on the Alkalive water source & quality.
  • Highlighting the purification process and premium look.
  • Encouraging engagement by asking what keeps followers balanced daily.

 

4. Seasonal Wellness & Education

Goal: To address season-specific hydration needs, focusing on the importance of staying properly hydrated even during cold weather, supporting energy and immunity.

What's new this month:

  • Content focusing on the Importance of hydration during winter.
  • Cozy winter aesthetic and seasonal cues to reinforce relevance.
  • Post scheduled for early January to kickstart the New Year's focus on health.

 

Notes:

To sustain consistency and ensure the brand maintains its bold yet human tone, all creative assets must adhere to the visual goal: Clean, airy, real-life Jordan moments, diverse, inclusive, and aspirational. The immediate focus remains on Launch Awareness and Education.

Title: Elite Recruitment - Q1 2026 - Social Media Marketing Strategy
Elite Property DXB - September 2025 - Social Media & Website Blog Strategy
strategy:
Objective:

 

Position Elite Property DXB as a trusted, aspirational real estate brand by:

  • Leading with lifestyle storytelling (buyers see their dream life).
  • Humanising Elite culture (agents see their dream career and are inspired to join, bringing in more agents).
  • Providing credible insights (everyone sees Elite as the authority).

Move away from salesy, cookie-cutter reels. Instead, create experiences, narratives, and insights that make Elite stand out.

 

Theme

Beyond the Property, It’s About the Lifestyle

 

Content Pillars

1. Lifestyle (Storytelling Around Properties & Communities)

 

Why? Because people buy into lifestyles, not just walls and roofs.

How? Create cinematic, story-led property and community tours that let viewers imagine themselves living there. Focus on moments, feelings, and experiences rather than just listing specs.

 

For Buyers: “I can see myself living there.”

For Agents: “I want to sell properties that feel like this.”

 

Examples:

Morning coffee on a balcony with a sea view.

Families enjoying the community park.

 

A walk-through that feels like a lifestyle ad, not a sales pitch.

 

2. Life at Elite (The Culture & Growth)

Why? Because Elite is more than a brokerage, it’s a lifestyle brand and career path.

How? Share human, relatable stories of the Elite team to attract talent and build trust.

 

Angles to explore:

Career Growth: Agents sharing their journey from where they started to where they are now.

Me Before vs. Me Now: Transformation stories (money, lifestyle, confidence).

Culture & Camaraderie: Show how Elite celebrates wins, supports agents, and creates a family-like environment.

 

For New Agents: “I want to be part of that team.”

For Buyers: “I trust agents who love where they work.”

 

3. Educational (Insights & Credibility)

Why? Because credibility = trust, and trust = clients + agents.

How? Share short, snackable insights and data points that position Elite as the go-to expert in Dubai’s real estate market.

 

Content formats:

 

Market Insights: “Why Palm Jumeirah homes are seeing record demand.”

Quick Facts: Bite-sized posts/reels with surprising property facts.

Tips: Buyer advice, agent growth tips, investment know-how.

 

For Buyers: “This brand knows what they’re talking about, I can trust them.”

For Agents: “This is a place where I can learn and grow.”

 

Grid & Aesthetic Direction

 

Visual Anchor: Lifestyle remains the hero. Properties and communities are shot in a cinematic, aspirational style.

Life at Elite content blends seamlessly, agents shown in the same aesthetic (so they look like the lifestyle, not just behind desks).

Educational content is presented clean, modern, and on-brand (quick hits of value, still visually elevated).

 

For Agents: “I want to be like them.”

For Buyers: “I want to live there.”

 

References

  • https://www.instagram.com/reel/DNBMK00I59j/?igsh=dzd5b2RuemVxZmVv
  • https://vt.tiktok.com/ZSAyugDX3/

Title: Crypto Homes DXB - August 2025 - Social Media Strategy
strategy:
In Month 2, the focus for Crypto Homes DXB is to grow brand visibility, credibility, and audience engagement by leveraging platform-specific strengths and delivering value-driven content. The strategy includes:

  • Establishing Crypto Homes DXB as a pioneer in crypto-powered real estate through consistent and clear messaging.
  • Prioritising Instagram and TikTok with short-form video content (Reels) that educates, entertains, and builds trust.
  • Delivering 8 Reels, 6 static/carousel posts, and 3 Stories per week to maintain consistency and boost algorithm visibility.
  • Highlighting Dubai real estate projects and crypto FAQs to attract a broader, curious audience.
  • Positioning the brand as credible on LinkedIn with insights, thought leadership content, and reposts from founders or team members.
  • Monitoring Facebook’s role as a repurposing or niche ad platform with minimal resource allocation.
  • Focusing on brand awareness and education only — no lead gen campaigns this month.

Title: Esperia Volleyball Academy - Q1 2026 - Social Media & Website Blogs Strategy
strategy:
Q1 2026 is about converting December visibility into long-term commitment. Esperia’s strategy will shift from seasonal hype to structured development storytelling, reinforcing EVA as a trusted academy where players grow week after week. By maintaining high-performing engagement content (challenges, team culture), doubling down on education (tutorials + coach expertise), and introducing volleyball news and fun facts, Esperia will position itself as the most knowledgeable, active, and community-driven volleyball academy in the region.

 

Quarterly Objectives

  1. Promote the campaign for the month
  2. Build parent trust through expertise and structure
  3. Increase teen & youth engagement via relatable, fun content
  4. Establish Esperia as a go-to volleyball authority in the UAE

 

Deliverables (Monthly Basis)

4 Reels

4 Static/Carousel Posts

8 Stories

 

(Scale up/down based on events, tournaments, or sign-up pushes)

 

Target Audience

Primary:

Parents of kids aged 6–17 in Dubai & Abu Dhabi seeking structured sports programs, skill development, and long-term training.

 

Secondary:

Teen athletes, young adults, volleyball enthusiasts, local volleyball community.

 

Core Content Pillars (Quarter-Wide)

1. Tutorials, Tips & How-Tos (Evergreen)

Goal: Establish authority and deliver value consistently.

 

Reels:

  • Quick demos
  • What-Not-To-Do formats
  • Before/After skill clips

 

Why it matters:

Educational content builds saves, shares, and long-term trust.

 

2. Meet the Coaches (Evergreen)

Goal: Humanise the brand and build confidence in coaching quality.

 

Reels:

  • Coach Spotlight
  • Coaches explaining drills
  • Warm-ups, corrections, encouragement moments

 

Why it matters:

Parents buy into people before programs.

 

3. Volleyball Trends/Challenges, Fun Moments & Team Culture (Evergreen)

Goal: Drive engagement and show the joy of training at EVA.

 

Reels:

  • Coach vs Student
  • Team challenges
  • Funny training moments

 

Why it matters:

This is your engagement engine and keeps the feed youthful and energetic.

 

4. Volleyball News, Market Trends & Fun Facts

Goal: Position Esperia as informed, current, and globally connected to the sport.

 

  • Volleyball Fun Fact of the Week
  • What’s Happening in World Volleyball
  • Rule explanations
  • Pro player highlights
  • Youth volleyball insights

 

Why it matters:

Builds credibility and gives parents confidence that EVA follows global standards.

 

Monthly Focus Breakdown

JANUARY – New Year, New Goals

Theme: Restart & Commit

 

Focus:

  • New term sign-ups
  • Back-to-training motivation
  • Goal-setting for players

 

Content Angle:

“Start 2026 Strong”

“Why Consistency Beats Talent”

“What Your Child Learns in Their First Month”

 

FEBRUARY – Progress & Development

Theme: Skill Growth & Structure

 

Focus:

  • Player improvement
  • Technique refinement
  • Coach expertise

 

Content Angle:

Progress-based tutorials

Skill breakdowns

Coach-led education

 

MARCH – Community & Momentum

Theme: Belonging & Confidence

 

Focus:

  • Team spirit
  • Player confidence
  • Community building

 

Content Angle:

Team highlights

Challenges

Testimonials & reactions

Title: Lino Lada - Q1 2026 - Social Media Marketing Strategy
strategy:
A. Objectives:

The primary objective for Q1 2026 is to boost consumption frequency by integrating Lino Lada into the daily lives of consumers through educational routines.

Additionally, the strategy aims to strengthen brand loyalty by fostering a positive and interactive community environment.

 

B. Strategy:

 

Following the holiday season, the Q1 strategy focuses on transitioning Lino Lada from a "seasonal indulgence" to an "everyday essential". This will be achieved through:

  1. Morning Routine Integration: Positioning the product as the perfect start to the day through specific "Morning Routine Cases".
  2. Educational Content: Providing clear, routine-based tutorials that show consumers how to incorporate Lino Lada into various meals, such as "Crunchy Chapathi".
  3. Community-First Approach: Shifting toward more interactive content to build a loyal, vocal community.

 

Content Deliverables:

  • Reels: Video-focused storytelling and tutorials.
  • Statics: High-quality product imagery and routine highlights.
  • Stories: Real-time community engagement and "Ask Me Anything" sessions.

 

Content Pillars:

 

1. Morning Routines & Consumption Frequency

  • Goal: To increase the frequency of use by showcasing Lino Lada as a staple of the morning routine.
  • What’s new this month:
    "Brighten Your Day":     Content focused on how Lino Lada makes mornings better and more flavorful.
    Routine-Based Posts: Showcasing specific use cases, such as pairing with chapathi or other breakfast favorites.

 

2. Community Engagement & Brand Loyalty

  • Goal: To build a stronger bond with the audience through active participation and community-driven content.
  • What’s new this month:
    "Ask Anything":     Interactive Q&A sessions to engage directly with the community.
    Community Highlights: Content that celebrates the "Lada community" and encourages positive interactions.

 

3. The Magic of Lino Lada (Product & Process)

  • Goal: To educate the consumer on the quality and "magic" behind the product's creation.
  • What’s new this month:
    "How We Make Magic":     Behind-the-scenes or process-oriented content showing the care put into Lino Lada.
    The "Spoon that Sings": Creative product placement focusing on the sensory experience of the product.

Title: Elite Vacations - Q1 2026 - Social Media Marketing Strategy
strategy:
The overarching strategy for Q1 2026 is to establish the refreshed Elite Vacations brand identity, set the new editorial visual tone, and build initial market awareness as the brand emerges with its own distinct vision.

 

Q1 Strategic Focus Areas:

  • Brand Identity: Formally launch the new premium, editorial aesthetic across social media.
  • Content Pillars: Deploy the "hero content pillars" to move away from traditional property listings toward lifestyle-led storytelling.

 

Tone:

Classy, sophisticated, composed.

 

The tone is polished and intentional; never rushed or loud, reflecting a premium travel mindset

 

Content Deliverables (Q1)

12 Reels

8 Statics/Carousels

 

Core Content Pillars:

1. The Traveller’s Perspective

Focuses on the sensory experience of arriving and living in the space. It’s about the "how it feels" rather than the "what is there." It captures the quiet routines and transitions of a premium traveler.

 

What it shows:

  • Arrival moments
  • Morning light
  • Quiet routines
  • Evening transitions

 

Why it exists:

Positions Elite Vacations as traveller-led, thoughtful, and experience-first.

 

2. Curated Stays as Atmosphere

Homes shown as environments, not listings. Removes the "real estate" feel and replaces it with an editorial focus on design and feel. No walkthroughs, just moods.

 

What it shows:

  • Texture, materials, light
  • Silence and space
  • Subtle movement

 

Rules:

  • No walkthroughs
  • No amenities lists
  • No captions about “what’s included”

 

3. Dubai Curated

Positions Elite Vacations as a curator of the city. This isn't a tourist guide; it is a sophisticated collection of neighborhood moods and seasonal rhythms for the cultured visitors.

 

What it shows:

  • Neighbourhood moods
  • Cultural moments
  • Events worth knowing
  • Everyday rituals

 

Tone:
Understated, informed, never instructional.

 

4. Editorial Perspective

Dedicated to the brand's "worldview." It uses short statements, travel philosophy, and negative space to make Elite Vacations feel sophisticated.

 

What it shows:

  • Short statements
  • Observations
  • Travel philosophy
